summary: MDM4U counting problems where order does NOT matter use combinations: the number of ways to choose r objects from n is nCr = n!/(r!(n−r)!). Think committees, teams, or lottery draws.
method: Decide whether order matters — if it does NOT, use combinations.
Identify n (objects available) and r (objects chosen).
Cancel factorials to simplify the arithmetic.
For probability, use combinations to count favourable and total outcomes.

example.title: Choose a committee of 3 from 10
given: a committee of 3, order does not matter
steps: Order does not matter, so use combinations with n = 10, r = 3.
